mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE < 5.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based demand expansion, which lifts equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and reduced recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns in >50% of historical windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained decline in the industrial production index signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, which compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive INDPRO declines in post-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are not statistically higher when GDPC1 growth exceeds 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5%, real borrowing costs rise and financial conditions tighten, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job growth exceeding 200,000 monthly and an unemployment rate below 5.5% confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle, which sustains consumer spending and equity earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in >55% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a divergence where industrial production declines while housing starts remain elevated signals sectoral imbalance and inventory buildup, which triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive during INDPRO/HOUST divergence in late-cycle periods.
